title: Add Facial review queue, montage, and export lineage
operator_request: The Facial app is used to sort mined images for LoRA training. Handshake needs review queues, montage/contact-sheet style inspection, and non-destructive export lineage connected to Ingest/CKC.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Review decisions must be attributable and reversible. Exports must write manifests and artifact refs without mutating or deleting source images.

scope.summary: Port Facial review sessions, parallel-safe shard claims, montage mapping, and Kohya/LoRA export lineage into Atelier Ingest.
base_scope: Create review sessions over an Ingest batch or Facial run artifact.
Support pass/reject/unsure decisions with actor attribution, timestamps, reasons, tags, and notes.
Support parallel agent shard claims so multiple models can review without overlapping the same item by accident.
Generate montage/contact-sheet artifacts with tile-to-image mapping and source refs.
Export accepted candidates with a manifest recording source, decision lineage, dedupe state, identity/quality artifacts, and output refs.
Keep originals read-only; export copies or manifests only.
acceptance_criteria: Review sessions use stable content-hash/source-ref image IDs.
Shard claims are disjoint, actor-attributed, expirable/recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Decisions persist as receipts and can be replayed into summary status.
Montage artifacts include tile-to-image mappings for Argus/operator inspection.
Export manifest records source candidates, decisions, dedupe/identity/quality artifacts, and exported files without mutating originals.
risks_and_controls: Risk: two agents decide the same item inconsistently. Control: shard claims and decision receipts must include actor and conflict status.
Risk: export loses source lineage. Control: manifest must include source refs, hashes, review receipts, and upstream artifact refs.
Risk: montage tile mapping becomes visually unverifiable. Control: tests must assert tile map and Argus-visible refs.
verification: Non-destructive source fixture test and frontend/Argus state assertion if UI is included.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.

handoff.note: Backend-first implementation started. active_mt_id remains MT-010 because that is the separate active media/linking lane; MT-028 is tracked directly in this contract and mt_index row.
